calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HELEN LEE'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND QUASH WRIT OF EXECUTION.

Appearances: Christopher Peters of the Fullman Firm (714.534.0050) appearing via Court Call for defendant and moving party; Peter Henry Liederman (510.681.6916) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. Defendant has not met the statutory requirements of CCP 473(b) or 473.5. As to 473(b), Defendant has not established that default was entered due to her "surprise." The term "surprise" as used in 473, refers to some condition or situation in which a party is unexpectedly placed to his injury, without any default or negligence of his own, which ordinary prudence could not have guarded against. (Hearn v. Howard (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1193, 1206.) Defendant has not established she acted with ordinary prudence. The affidavit of the process server states that he properly effectuated substituted service, by serving a competent member of the household and subsequently mailing the documents. Defendant has not established that she did not live at the residence at the time of service or that she did not regularly receive mail there. For the same reasons, Defendant has not established that her lack of actual notice was not caused by her avoidance of service or inexcusable neglect, as required by CCP 473.5. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-26-2013 Continued To Oct-24-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT HELEN LEE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT is continued to October 24, 2013 to give the moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 26, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the [moving/opposition/reply] papers. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)
